name: Santuario Virgen de la Cabeza y embalse de Encinarejo - circular por el Parque Natural de la Sierra de Andújar
region_intro: Mountain biking around National Park Sierra de Andújar offers diverse terrain within the Sierra Morena, characterized by gentle undulations and altitudes ranging from 400 to 1,290 meters. The landscape features extensive Mediterranean forests, including holm oak, cork oak, and gall oak, alongside rivers like the Yeguas and Jándula, which feed into significant reservoirs. This varied environment provides a range of mountain bike trails suitable for different skill levels. The park is also known for its rich biodiversity and cultural sites, such as the Basilica and Royal Sanctuary of the Virgen de la Cabeza. Best mountain bike trails around National Park Sierra de Andújar The most popular mountain bike trail is Santuario Virgen de la Cabeza y embalse de Encinarejo - circular por el Parque Natural de la Sierra de Andújar, a difficult 27.1 miles (43.7 km) trail that takes 4 hours 21 minutes to complete. This route passes by the historic Sanctuary of the Virgen de la Cabeza and the Embalse del Encinarejo. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Cardeña, Azuel – Parque Natural Sierra de Cardeña y Montoro – ruta circular, a difficult 43.7 miles (70.3 km) path. This route explores the adjacent natural park, showcasing diverse Mediterranean forest landscapes. Local mountain bikers also love the Marmolejo Spa – San Ginés Hermitage loop from Andújar, a 40.5 miles (65.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain and cultural sites, often completed in about 6 hours 6 minutes. Mountain biking around National Park Sierra de Andújar is defined by Mediterranean forests, river valleys, and gentle hills. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in National Park Sierra de Andújar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 1000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore National Park Sierra de Andújar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in National Park Sierra de Andújar?
A: There are over 90 mountain bike trails in and around National Park Sierra de Andújar. These routes cater to various skill levels, with 19 easy, 52 moderate, and 22 difficult options.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in National Park Sierra de Andújar?
A: Yes, the park offers 19 easy mountain bike trails, perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride. These routes typically have minimal elevation gain and are shorter in duration, allowing you to enjoy the Mediterranean forest landscapes at a comfortable pace.

Q: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see while mountain biking in Sierra de Andújar?
A: National Park Sierra de Andújar is renowned for its rich biodiversity. You might spot endangered species like the Iberian lynx, wolves, and imperial eagles. Other common sightings include red deer, wild boar, wild cats, and mongooses. The park is also home to 178 bird species, including black vultures and golden eagles, making it excellent for wildlife observation.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike trails in the area?
A: Yes, many routes in National Park Sierra de Andújar are circular. For example, the moderate route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10319598" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Carreta on the Old Road – San Ginés Hermitage loop from Andújar&lt;/a&gt; is a 41.7 km circular trail that takes approximately 3 hours 28 minutes to complete.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Several trails pass by significant landmarks. You can visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1279995" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin of the Cabeza&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4484492" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Ginés Hermitage&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2862464" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Encinarejo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The park also features scenic viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5061639" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valley Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1278398" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Pastores Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, offering stunning panoramas of the landscape.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in National Park Sierra de Andújar?
A: The park is suitable for mountain biking year-round, but spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) offer the most pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Autumn is particularly special for observing the red deer rutting season. Summers can be hot, so early morning rides are recommended, while winters are generally mild.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that offer great views?
A: Absolutely. The park is known for its numerous spectacular viewpoints. Many trails traverse areas with elevated positions, providing panoramic vistas of the Mediterranean forests and river valleys. Look for routes that pass by named viewpoints such as El Tamujar, Migorramos, and El Peregrino, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5061639" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valley Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1278398" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Pastores Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in National Park Sierra de Andújar?
A: The mountain bike trails in National Park Sierra de Andújar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 1000 mountain bikers have explored the varied terrain, often praising the well-preserved Mediterranean forests, diverse landscapes, and the sense of immersion in nature.

Q: Are there any moderate mountain bike trails for intermediate riders?
A: Yes, the park has 52 moderate mountain bike trails, ideal for intermediate riders. These routes offer a good balance of challenge and enjoyment. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27628020" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marmolejo – Marmolejo Spa loop from Andújar&lt;/a&gt;, a 55.1 km trail that typically takes around 4 hours 4 minutes to complete.

Q: Is public transport available to access the mountain bike trails?
A: While public transport options directly into the heart of the National Park Sierra de Andújar are limited, you can often reach nearby towns like Andújar by bus. From there, you might need to arrange local transport or cycle to the trailheads. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for the most up-to-date information.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: Experienced riders will find 22 difficult mountain bike trails in the area. These routes often feature significant elevation changes and longer distances, sometimes requiring riders to push their bikes. The park's diverse terrain, including gentle undulations and higher peaks, provides a good challenge for advanced mountain bikers.
tips: It is a place of worship that venerates the Virgen de la Cabeza whose pilgrimage is celebrated every year on the last Sunday of April
It is a hermitage of the same name and a resting place for pilgrims on the way up to the Sanctuary of the Virgin of the Head. From here a descent begins until the passage through the Jándula River.

The reservoir is located in the Sierra de Andújar Natural Park, Jaén province, and receives water from the Jándula reservoir, located only seven kilometers to the north. It is one of the oldest in the Guadalquivir basin, inaugurated during the Second Republic.

waypoints.name: Basilica of the Sanctuary of the Virgin of the Head
